feature_request: Add a layered test verification workflow that provides an under-1-minute fast feedback lane and reorganizes ordinary PR/verify feedback toward a 5-minute target without weakening the project's quality contract.

repo_truth.freshness_evidence: .specify/project-cognition/status.json reports freshness: fresh, baseline_state: ready, last_refresh_reason: map-build

existing_commands.check:desktop: cd desktop && bun run lint && bun run test -- --run && bun run build

must_preserve.claim: Make this project's tests lighter and faster while preserving high-quality signal.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Feature must frame around speed plus quality, not speed alone.

must_preserve.claim: First scope adds an under-1-minute fast lane and reorganizes ordinary PR/verify toward a 5-minute target.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Spec must include both local fast feedback and PR/verify layering.

must_preserve.claim: Preferred technical direction is Option B: Layered PR Gate using existing impact-report/change-policy/quality-gate architecture.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Spec must reuse or extend existing gate architecture.

must_preserve.claim: Fast-lane evidence should be changed-area high-signal unit tests plus a tiny fixed core smoke set.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Spec must define how changed-area tests and core smoke tests are selected.

must_preserve.claim: Preserve bun run verify / quality:pr as the PR readiness entrypoint.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Spec must avoid replacing the existing readiness command with an incompatible path.

must_preserve.claim: E2E, live-provider, and release-gate evidence should be conditional for ordinary PR/verify, not removed.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Spec must define risk triggers for these checks.

must_preserve.claim: High quality means critical-path regression protection, low flaky rate, and changed-area coverage.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Spec acceptance criteria must use this quality definition.

must_preserve.claim: Do not weaken coverage baselines, thresholds, same-area test policy, persistence checks, quarantine governance, or release confidence without explicit approval.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Spec must preserve quality contracts and mark any policy change as explicit.

must_preserve.claim: Existing source-grounded surfaces include package.json, scripts/quality-gate/modes.ts, runner.ts, coverage.ts, scripts/pr/impact-report.ts, and scripts/pr/change-policy.ts.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Spec must cite these as likely implementation surfaces.

must_preserve.claim: Fast-lane success is a development signal, not full PR readiness.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Spec must require command/report wording that prevents false readiness claims.

must_preserve.claim: Expansion-ready test impact graph is deferred; start with layered PR gate first.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: Spec must keep first slice medium-complexity.

must_preserve.claim: Exact full-coverage handling under the 5-minute PR target is a soft planning question; coverage must not be weakened silently.
must_preserve.downstream_requirement: sp-specify or sp-plan must decide coverage strategy with explicit acceptance criteria.

users_and_roles.description: Developers making local changes who need fast feedback
Project maintainers reviewing PRs and ensuring quality
AI agents that need fast but trustworthy verification before handoff

in_scope: Add a fast lane targeting seconds to under 1 minute.
Reorganize ordinary bun run verify / quality:pr toward a 5-minute target for routine changes.
Reuse existing impact-report, change-policy, and quality-gate lane architecture.
Use changed-area high-signal unit tests plus a tiny fixed core smoke set as fast-lane evidence.
Keep E2E, live-provider, and release-gate evidence conditional for ordinary PR/verify.
Preserve quality contracts around same-area tests, quarantine governance, persistence upgrade checks, coverage policy, and release confidence.
out_of_scope: Full expansion-ready test impact graph in the first slice.
Deleting tests merely to reduce runtime.
Weakening coverage baselines or thresholds without explicit maintainer approval.
Making live-provider, broad E2E, or release gates part of the default fast lane.
Replacing bun run verify as the PR readiness entrypoint.
acceptance_signals: A routine local change can run a fast lane in seconds to under 1 minute.
Ordinary PR/verify has a credible path toward under 5 minutes for non-high-risk changes.
Fast-lane routing is explained by changed files and risk notes.
High-risk paths still trigger heavier checks.
E2E/live-provider/release evidence is retained as conditional confidence.
Reports and command output clearly distinguish fast feedback from PR readiness.
Regression tests prove lane routing does not skip required checks for desktop, server, adapter, docs, native, provider/runtime, and release-sensitive paths.
critical_constraints: Must preserve bun run verify as PR readiness entrypoint.
Must not weaken coverage policy silently.
Must not bypass same-area test requirements.
Fast-lane success is NOT merge-ready signal.

soft_unknowns.question: Exact handling of full coverage in the 5-minute ordinary PR target.
soft_unknowns.risk: Coverage strategy must not weaken ratchets/baselines without explicit approval.
